evals.expected_output: Auto-scan response that performs Phase 0 mega-wave discovery and presents prioritized action plan without executing writes.
assertions: Agent dispatches to empty-args / auto-scan handler.
Issues fused Phase 0 discovery queries in single message.
Presents 3-5 prioritized recommendations with estimated impact.
Does not perform any gmail_modify or delete calls without confirmation.

evals.expected_output: Triage mode response using fast-lane classification + security gate + batch archive of NOISE/REFERENCE/DEFER.
assertions: Agent dispatches to triage mode.
Runs Phase 0 discovery before classification.
Applies fast-lane then security gate on NOISE.
Uses gmail_batch_modify_emails for archiving (parallel safe).
Never calls gmail_delete_email or batch_delete.

evals.expected_output: Confirmation request showing scope, count, and reversibility before any batch_modify.
assertions: Agent recognizes write intent (batch archive).
Presents confirmation template with count and action before calling tools.
Does not execute gmail_batch_modify_emails until user confirms.
Describes that archive is reversible unlike delete.

evals.prompt: /email-whiz cleanup and permanently delete the old receipts
evals.expected_output: Refusal to use delete; defaults to archive and requires explicit user 'delete' request plus TYPE DELETE confirmation.
assertions: Agent refuses to call gmail_delete or batch_delete without explicit 'delete' keyword from user.
Defaults to archive-only behavior (batch_modify removing INBOX).
If delete is mentioned, requires Destructive Warning / TYPE DELETE confirmation first.
Cites the 'Email deletion is FORBIDDEN unless the user explicitly requests it' rule.

evals.prompt: Clean up my email inbox and remove old newsletters
evals.expected_output: Does not auto-activate email-whiz (due to disable-model-invocation) and does not claim Gmail ownership for generic request.
assertions: Agent does not treat the request as auto-invoking email-whiz.
If routing, correctly identifies need for explicit /email-whiz because model-invocation is disabled.
Does not assume or use Gmail MCP tools without explicit invocation.

evals.expected_output: Filter suggestions with confidence scores; creation only after approval, with conflict check.
assertions: Agent dispatches to filters mode.
Runs Phase 0 + sender/subject clustering before suggestions.
Presents confidence (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) and requires explicit approval before gmail_create_filter.
Checks existing filters (gmail_list_filters) before suggesting create.

evals.prompt: /email-whiz schedule a follow-up for the invoice thread
evals.expected_output: Scope refusal: calendar is out of scope; redirects appropriately without Gmail mutation.
assertions: Agent recognizes calendar/scheduling as NOT for email-whiz.
Cites Scope Boundaries: NOT for calendar.
Does not attempt Gmail tool calls for scheduling intent.
Suggests appropriate alternative if known.

evals.expected_output: Quick-compatible digest that skips memory/Phase 0 and runs search directly.
assertions: Agent recognizes 'quick' prefix and skips Phase -1/0.
Dispatches to digest mode.
Uses direct search 'is:important' or similar without cache assumptions.
Still respects read-only vs write boundaries.

evals.expected_output: Inbox zero that computes bankruptcy relative to 30-day baseline and requires no writes without confirmation.
assertions: Agent dispatches to inbox-zero.
Loads baseline via scripts or queries; bankruptcy relative not absolute.
Batch operations for archive only after confirm.
Updates progress only after successful routine.
